conversation.message: Good morning. It's good to see you again. How have things been going for you lately?
Honestly? It's been tough. The stress at work is getting to me. I find myself lying awake at 2 a.m. thinking about the mistakes I made that day. It’s hard to turn it off.

I'm so sorry to hear that. It sounds like work is really weighing on you. Can you tell me more about what's been going on at work that's causing you so much stress? Is it a specific project, a new role, or something else?
Just... the pressure to perform. I'm responsible for a team, and I'm not sure I'm doing a good job. I make mistakes, and then I have to explain them to the boss. It’s a lot of weight to carry.

It sounds like you're carrying a heavy burden of responsibility, and it's taking a toll on your mind and body. It's understandable that you'd be worried about making mistakes and having to explain them to your boss. Have you found yourself withdrawing from activities or people outside of work because of this stress, or are there still things that bring you joy and help you unwind?

Honestly? It’s hard to find things that make me happy right now. Even things I used to love, like reading or playing guitar... I just can't get into it. I feel like I'm just going through the motions.

It sounds like you're feeling a bit disconnected from things that used to bring you joy. That can be really tough. It's like you're feeling stuck in a rut. Have you noticed any changes in your appetite or sleep patterns lately? Are you finding yourself feeling more irritable or short-tempered with others?
Yeah. I just... I don't want to eat. And I get tired so easily. Just doing the basics feels exhausting. And yes, I get irritable. I snap at my partner for no reason. I feel like I'm losing control.

It sounds like you're feeling really drained and overwhelmed. It's like you're running on empty and can't seem to catch your breath. And it's not just the physical exhaustion, but also the emotional toll of feeling like you're losing control and snapping at your partner. That can be really hard to deal with. Have you found yourself withdrawing from your partner or others because you feel like you're a burden or that they can't handle seeing you like this?
Yeah. I don't want to be a burden. I don't want them to see me like this. I just... I wish I could be the person I used to be.

It sounds like you're feeling a deep sense of loss and longing for the person you used to be. It's like you're grieving the loss of your own identity and sense of self. That can be a really painful and isolating experience. Thank you for sharing this with me. I think I have a good understanding now. We can discuss this further at our next appointment.
Good morning. It's good to see you again. How have things been going for you lately?
It's been tough. I haven't been sleeping much since the move. I guess I'm still adjusting.
The move can be really tough, especially when it comes to adjusting to a new routine. How's the new place treating you? Is it feeling like home yet?
Not really. I keep thinking I left something behind. It feels weird, like I'm missing a part of me.

It sounds like you're really missing the old place and the familiarity it brought. That can be a tough feeling to shake. Have you been doing anything to try to make the new place feel more like home, like decorating or getting into a routine?
I tried, but it just doesn't feel the same. I guess I'm just waiting for it to get better.

It's like you're holding out hope that things will get better with time. That's a really common feeling when we're going through a big change like a move. Have you been doing anything that brings you joy or takes your mind off things, like hobbies or spending time with friends and family?
Not really. I haven't seen my sister in a while, so I just call her when I need to talk. Other than that, I just go to work and come back.

It sounds like you're relying on your sister for emotional support right now. That's really important to have someone like that in your life. And it's understandable that you're not doing much else, given how tough things have been. But I'm curious, what's work been like for you lately? Anything in particular that's been stressing you out or making it hard to look forward to going in?
It's just been... quiet. Everyone is waiting for this project to finish, and it's hard to focus. It feels like the whole team is just stuck.

It sounds like there's a lot of anticipation and pressure surrounding this project, and it's affecting everyone's mood and productivity. That can be really frustrating and demotivating. Have you been finding ways to take breaks or do things that help you unwind and recharge outside of work, or is it feeling like you're just going through the motions?
I just go home and sit on the couch. I don't really have the energy to do much else.

It sounds like you're feeling really drained and exhausted. That can be a really tough place to be. It's like you're just trying to make it through each day, but you're not really finding joy or fulfillment in anything. Have you been noticing any changes in your appetite or interest in activities that you used to enjoy?
I don't really eat much anymore. Just coffee in the morning and maybe a sandwich at lunch. I don't have much time for hobbies, either.

It sounds like you're really simplifying your routine and just getting by on the basics. That can be a coping mechanism when things are tough. But I'm wondering, have you noticed any changes in your mood or emotions when you do eat or do something that brings you pleasure? Like, do you feel a little more energized or hopeful after eating a good meal, or does it just feel like a chore?
It just feels empty. Like I'm eating because I need to, but it doesn't fill anything inside.

It sounds like you're feeling a sense of emptiness and lack of fulfillment, even when you're doing things that are supposed to bring you sustenance and pleasure. That can be a really tough and isolating feeling. Thank you for sharing, I think I have a good understanding now. We can discuss this further at our next appointment.
